It was established in February 1964 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

**The Most Distinguished Order of Paduka Seri Laila Jasa**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Paduka Seri Laila Jasa Yang Amat Berjasa*), also translated as **The Most Distinguished Order of Merit of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established in February 1964 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Current classes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Paduka_Seri_Laila_Jasa&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current classes")]

The three classes of appointment to the Order are, from highest grade to lowest grade:

| Class | [Post-nominal](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") | [Title](/wiki/Malay_styles_and_titles "Malay styles and titles") | [Ribbon bar](/wiki/Ribbon_bar "Ribbon bar") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First Class | PSLJ | Dato Paduka Seri Laila Jasa |  |
| Second Class | DSLJ | Dato Seri Laila Jasa |  |
| Third Class | SLJ | – |  |

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Paduka_Seri_Laila_Jasa&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Description")]

The first class included 14 medallions measuring 1,100 millimetres (43 in) in silver, gold, and enamel, along with the badge appendant, measuring 60 millimetres (2.4 in) in silver, gold, and enamel; the star, measuring 83 millimetres (3.3 in) in silver, gold, and enamel, with a gold retaining pin and the maker's cartouche on the reverse, and presented in a fitted leather case with a miniature award.[[3]](#cite_note-:8-3)

The pre-1984 first class consisted of a badge and star with two lions on them. The second class adopted the Bruneian crown in place of lions when Brunei gained [official independence from Britain](/wiki/National_Day_(Brunei) "National Day (Brunei)") in 1984.[[3]](#cite_note-:8-3)

## Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Paduka_Seri_Laila_Jasa&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Recipients")]

### First Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Paduka_Seri_Laila_Jasa&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: First Class")]

* Unknown – [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") – [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei")[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4)
* Unknown – [Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Omar Ali Saifuddien III") – Sultan of Brunei[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4)
* Unknown – [Pengiran Anak Kemaluddin Al-Haj](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Kemaluddin_Al-Haj "Pengiran Anak Kemaluddin Al-Haj") – [Speaker of Legislative Council](/wiki/Speaker_of_Legislative_Council_of_Brunei "Speaker of Legislative Council of Brunei")[[5]](#cite_note-:13-5)
* Unknown – [Marianne E. Lloyd-Dolbey](/wiki/Marianne_Elisabeth_Lloyd-Dolbey "Marianne Elisabeth Lloyd-Dolbey") – [Personal Secretary](/wiki/Personal_assistant "Personal assistant") to [Sultan](/wiki/List_of_sultans_of_Brunei "List of sultans of Brunei") [Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Omar Ali Saifuddien III")[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* Unknown – [Pengiran Umar](/wiki/Pengiran_Umar "Pengiran Umar") – [Commissioner of Police](/wiki/Police_commissioner "Police commissioner")[[7]](#cite_note-:4-7)
* Unknown – [Pengiran Muhammad Ali](/wiki/Pengiran_Muhammad_Ali "Pengiran Muhammad Ali") – Deputy Menteri Besar[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* Unknown – [Ahmad Wally Skinner](/wiki/Ahmad_Wally_Skinner "Ahmad Wally Skinner") – [Deputy Minister of Finance](/wiki/Ministry_of_Finance_and_Economy_(Brunei) "Ministry of Finance and Economy (Brunei)")[[9]](#cite_note-:14-9)
* 1966 – Sir Stephen Luke – Senior Crown Agent[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* 2002 – [Ghazali Shafie](/wiki/Ghazali_Shafie "Ghazali Shafie") – Malaysian [Minister of Foreign Affairs](/wiki/Minister_of_Foreign_Affairs_(Malaysia) "Minister of Foreign Affairs (Malaysia)")[[11]](#cite_note-:9-11)
* 2023 – [Tengah Ali Hasan](/wiki/Awang_Tengah_Ali_Hasan "Awang Tengah Ali Hasan") – [Deputy Premier of Sarawak](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Sarawak "Cabinet of Sarawak")[[12]](#cite_note-12)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Dato_and_Datin_Lloyd-Dolbey.jpg/250px-Dato_and_Datin_Lloyd-Dolbey.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dato_and_Datin_Lloyd-Dolbey.jpg)

Dato and Datin Lloyd-Dolbey with their respective orders in 1967

### Second Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Paduka_Seri_Laila_Jasa&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Second Class")]

* Unknown – Major General [Husin](/wiki/Husin_Ahmad "Husin Ahmad") – [Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces](/wiki/Commander_of_the_Royal_Brunei_Armed_Forces "Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces")[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* Unknown – Major General [Jaafar](/wiki/Jaafar_Abdul_Aziz "Jaafar Abdul Aziz") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* Unknown – Lieutenant Colonel [Musa](/wiki/Musa_Yakub "Musa Yakub") – Commander of the Training Institute[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* Unknown – Colonel [Jocklin](/wiki/Jocklin_Kongpaw "Jocklin Kongpaw") – Commanding Officer of Air Wing ABDB*[[16]](#cite_note-:12-16)*
* Unknown – [Pengiran Anak Besar](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Besar "Pengiran Anak Besar") – Nobility[[17]](#cite_note-:02-17)
* Unknown – [Zakaria Sulaiman](/wiki/Zakaria_Sulaiman "Zakaria Sulaiman") – Minister of Communications[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* Unknown – Major General [Pengiran Ibnu Basit](/wiki/Pengiran_Ibnu_Basit "Pengiran Ibnu Basit") – Commander of the [Royal Brunei Navy](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Navy "Royal Brunei Navy")[[19]](#cite_note-:2-19)
* Unknown – [Ahmad Jumat](/wiki/Ahmad_Jumat "Ahmad Jumat") – [Minister of Culture, Youth and Sports](/wiki/Ministry_of_Culture,_Youth_and_Sports_(Brunei) "Ministry of Culture, Youth and Sports (Brunei)")[[20]](#cite_note-:26-20)
* Unknown – Raoul Teesdale Lloyd-Dolbey – British officer[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* Unknown – [Ismail Omar Abdul Aziz](/wiki/Ismail_Omar_Abdul_Aziz "Ismail Omar Abdul Aziz") – [State Mufti](/wiki/State_Mufti_of_Brunei "State Mufti of Brunei")[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* Unknown – [James Richard Henry Burns](/wiki/James_Richard_Henry_Burns "James Richard Henry Burns") – [Commissioner of Police](/wiki/Police_commissioner "Police commissioner")[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* Unknown – [Ya'akub Zainal](/wiki/Ya%27akub_Zainal "Ya'akub Zainal") – Commissioner of Police[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* 1966 – [Alfred Suenson-Taylor](/wiki/Alfred_Suenson-Taylor,_1st_Baron_Grantchester "Alfred Suenson-Taylor, 1st Baron Grantchester") – head of investment banking Asia Pacific region[[25]](#cite_note-:5-25)
* 1966 – [Daniel Knox](/wiki/Daniel_Knox,_6th_Earl_of_Ranfurly "Daniel Knox, 6th Earl of Ranfurly") – former [governor of the Bahamas](/wiki/Governor_of_the_Bahamas "Governor of the Bahamas")[[25]](#cite_note-:5-25)
* 1966 – William Henry Doughty – oldest European and the longest resident of Brunei[[25]](#cite_note-:5-25)
* 1967 – [Abdul Rahman Taha](/wiki/Abdul_Rahman_Taha "Abdul Rahman Taha") – Member of Privy Council[[26]](#cite_note-:333-26)
* 1968 – William lan Glass – Controller of Civil Aviation and Establishment Officer[[27]](#cite_note-:22-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)
* 1969 – [Zain Serudin](/wiki/Zain_Serudin "Zain Serudin") – [Minister of Religious Affairs](/wiki/Ministry_of_Religious_Affairs_(Brunei) "Ministry of Religious Affairs (Brunei)")[[29]](#cite_note-:022-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* 1969 – Malcolm MacInnes – Director of Education[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 1969 – P. A. Coates – Member of [Privy Council](/wiki/Privy_Council_(Brunei) "Privy Council (Brunei)")[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 1969 – T. E. Owen – Superintendent of Police[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 1969 – [William John Peel](/wiki/John_Peel_(Leicester_MP) "John Peel (Leicester MP)") – British Resident to Brunei[[32]](#cite_note-:04-32)
* 1971 – [John Vivian](/wiki/John_Vivian,_4th_Baron_Swansea "John Vivian, 4th Baron Swansea") – British aristocrat[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* 1978 – Yussof Mohd Limbang – Member of Privy Council[[32]](#cite_note-:04-32)
* 1979 – [Lukan Uking](/wiki/Lukan_Uking "Lukan Uking") – [Member of the Legislative Council](/wiki/Legislative_Council_of_Brunei "Legislative Council of Brunei")[[34]](#cite_note-34)
* 1983 – [Abdul Aziz Juned](/wiki/Abdul_Aziz_Juned "Abdul Aziz Juned") – [State Mufti](/wiki/State_Mufti_of_Brunei "State Mufti of Brunei")[[35]](#cite_note-35)
* 1986 – [Pengiran Anak Puteh](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Puteh "Pengiran Anak Puteh") – [Diplomat](/wiki/Diplomat "Diplomat") and nobility[[36]](#cite_note-:03-36)
* 1989 – [Abu Bakar Apong](/wiki/Abu_Bakar_Apong "Abu Bakar Apong") – [Minister of Home Affairs](/wiki/Minister_of_Home_Affairs "Minister of Home Affairs")[[37]](#cite_note-:32-37)
* 2003 – Anwarullah Shafiullah – Member Islamic Law Expert at the Ministry of Religious Affairs[[38]](#cite_note-38)
* 2010 – Rodman R. Bundy – Partner in [Eversheds litigation](/wiki/Eversheds_Sutherland "Eversheds Sutherland")[[39]](#cite_note-:42-39)
* 2010 – [Alan Vaughan Lowe](/wiki/Vaughan_Lowe "Vaughan Lowe") – [Chichele Professor of Public International Law](/wiki/Chichele_Professor_of_Public_International_Law "Chichele Professor of Public International Law") in the [University of Oxford](/wiki/University_of_Oxford "University of Oxford")[[39]](#cite_note-:42-39)
* 2011 – [Peter Voser](/wiki/Peter_Voser "Peter Voser") – [CEO](/wiki/Chief_executive_officer "Chief executive officer") of [Royal Dutch Shell](/wiki/Shell_plc "Shell plc")[[40]](#cite_note-:16-40)
* 2011 – [Malcolm Brinded](/wiki/Malcolm_Brinded "Malcolm Brinded") – Executive Board Member of [Royal Dutch Shell](/wiki/Shell_plc "Shell plc")[[40]](#cite_note-:16-40)
* 2011 – Babu Sukamaran – Medical Director of [Pantai Jerudong Specialist Centre](/wiki/Pantai_Jerudong_Specialist_Centre "Pantai Jerudong Specialist Centre")[[40]](#cite_note-:16-40)
* 2011 – Uta Meyding-Lamade – Help set up the [JPMC](/wiki/Jerudong_Park_Medical_Centre "Jerudong Park Medical Centre") Neuroscience Stroke and Rehabilitation Centre[[40]](#cite_note-:16-40)
* 2012 – Shamsul Azhar Abbas – President and CEO of [Petronas](/wiki/Petronas "Petronas")[[41]](#cite_note-:6-41)
* 2012 – [Ken Kobayashi](/wiki/Ken_Kobayashi "Ken Kobayashi") – CEO of [Mitsubishi Corporation](/wiki/Mitsubishi_Corporation "Mitsubishi Corporation")[[41]](#cite_note-:6-41)
* 2012 – Maliakel John Alexander – Gastroenterology Specialist[[41]](#cite_note-:6-41)
* 2014 – [Ahmad Fuzi Abdul Razak](/wiki/Ahmad_Fuzi_Abdul_Razak "Ahmad Fuzi Abdul Razak") – Secretary General of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Malaysia[[42]](#cite_note-:7-42)
* 2014 – Rastam Mohd Isa – Secretary General of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Malaysia[[43]](#cite_note-43)
* 2018 – [James Kerr Findlay](/wiki/James_Kerr_Findlay "James Kerr Findlay") – [Commissioner of the Supreme Court](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_Brunei_Darussalam "Supreme Court of Brunei Darussalam")[[44]](#cite_note-44)

* 1969 – [Judin Asar](/wiki/Judin_Asar "Judin Asar") – Clerk to the Legislative Council[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 1969 – G. R. Crowhurst – Music Director of Royal Brunei Police Force Band[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 1969 – Damit Metussin – Nobility[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 1969 – Garaham Salleck – [Qantas](/wiki/Qantas "Qantas") employee to manage the travel needs for Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 1969 – H. J. R. Bennett – Head of [Prison Department](/wiki/Brunei_Prison_Department "Brunei Prison Department")[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31)
* 2011 – Shaukat Zinna – Medical practitioner[[40]](#cite_note-:16-40)
* 2013 – Haroon Manadath Pareed Pillay – Specialist and Head of Department of Neurosurgery at [RIPAS Hospital](/wiki/Raja_Isteri_Pengiran_Anak_Saleha_Hospital "Raja Isteri Pengiran Anak Saleha Hospital")[[53]](#cite_note-:132-53)[[54]](#cite_note-:15-54)
* 2014 – Meyyappan Nadarajan Thevar – Contributions to the RBAF Command and Staff College[[42]](#cite_note-:7-42)[[55]](#cite_note-55)

### Former recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Paduka_Seri_Laila_Jasa&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Former recipients")]

* Unknown – Nawawi Taha – [Personal and Confidential Secretary](/wiki/Personal_assistant "Personal assistant") to [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah")[[56]](#cite_note-56)[[57]](#cite_note-57)
